core_mechanism: Position-dependent interpolation (a partition-of-unity / smooth spatial weighting that blends two distinct governing vector fields into one), together with the integrability question of whether the resulting coordinate-dependent mixture of gradient fields can itself be written as the gradient of a single scalar potential — the obstruction being that such a mixture is generically non-conservative (path-dependent).
brief_reason: The abstract contrasts a force-based versus a potential-energy-based way of smoothly switching between two descriptions via auxiliary 'switching' functions and argues the potential-energy route cannot yield a robust energy-conserving scheme, i.e. the spatially interpolated force field is not derivable from one conserved scalar potential.
